{{Short description="Rotational analogue of linear momentum}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Angular momentum
| image = Gyroscope operation.gif
| caption = A [[gyroscope]] remains upright while spinning due to conservation of angular momentum.
| topics = [[Classical mechanics]], [[Momentum]], [[Spin (physics)]], [[Noether's theorem]]
| symbol = L
| SI_unit = kg·m²/s
| conserved = Yes (in rotationally symmetric systems)
}}

'''Angular momentum''' (sometimes called '''moment of momentum''' or '''rotational momentum''') is the [[rotational]] analog of linear [[momentum]]. It is a [[vector quantity]] that represents the product of a body's [[moment of inertia|rotational inertia]] and [[angular velocity|rotational velocity]] about a particular axis.

For a point particle:
:<math>\mathbf{L} = \mathbf{r} \times \mathbf{p} = \mathbf{r} \times m\mathbf{v}</math>

where <math>\mathbf{r}</math> is the position vector relative to the reference point and <math>\mathbf{p}</math> is the linear momentum.

== Conservation ==
{{Main|Conservation of angular momentum}}
Angular momentum is conserved in a system where there is no net external [[torque]]. This is a consequence of rotational symmetry via [[Noether's theorem]]:

:<math>\frac{d\mathbf{L}}{dt} = \boldsymbol{\tau}_{\text{ext}}</math>

If <math>\boldsymbol{\tau}_{\text{ext}} = 0</math>, then <math>\mathbf{L} = \text{constant}</math>.

== Classical mechanics ==
For a rigid body rotating about a fixed axis:
:<math>L = I\omega</math>

where <math>I</math> is the [[moment of inertia]] and <math>\omega</math> is the [[angular velocity]].

=== Orbital vs. spin angular momentum ===
* '''Orbital''' — motion of the centre of mass about a point
* '''Spin''' — rotation about the centre of mass

== Quantum mechanics ==
In [[quantum mechanics]], angular momentum is quantised:

:<math>L^2 = \ell(\ell+1)\hbar^2, \quad \ell = 0, 1, 2, \ldots</math>
:<math>L_z = m_\ell\hbar, \quad m_\ell = -\ell, -\ell+1, \ldots, +\ell</math>

[[Spin (physics)|Spin angular momentum]] is an intrinsic property of particles:
:<math>S^2 = s(s+1)\hbar^2</math>

== Applications ==
* [[Kepler's laws of planetary motion|Kepler's second law]] — equal areas in equal times (conservation of angular momentum)
* Figure skating — spin rate increases when arms are pulled in (decrease of <math>I</math>)
* [[Gyroscope]] — maintains orientation due to angular momentum
* [[Neutron star]] rotation — conservation from stellar collapse
* [[Atomic orbital]]s — quantised angular momentum shapes

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Moment of inertia]]
* [[Momentum]]
* [[Noether's theorem]]
* [[Spin (physics)]]
* [[Torque]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Angular momentum| ]]
[[Category:Classical mechanics]]
[[Category:Physical quantities]]
